title: tephroseris papposa subsp. wolffii
intro: Tephroseris papposa subsp. wolffii, also known as the Mountain Everlasting, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to have various medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Mountain Everlasting include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular natural remedy for various ailments. The herbal preparations of Mountain Everlasting typically involve drying the plant's flowers and leaves, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
